name: Pathfinder returns NO path when toTokens=[score group] but the sink cannot receive it (P11 sink-only, real data)
description: Real-data regression guard for the toTokens-enforcement fix and a P11 (sink-only) negative control. Block-pinned projection tier via ProxyLoadGraph at 46488973, where the score group is fully active (see score-group-path-mint-through-router-001, which mints it for a sink that DOES trust it). Here the sink (0x742b7dbb) is a registered avatar that does NOT trust the score group. Because the score group's unwrapped token is wrapped-only / sink-only (it can only reach a recipient via a fresh group mint, and the mint edge requires the sink to trust the group), the sink cannot receive the score group token at all — so toTokens=[score group] is unsatisfiable and the only correct result is NO path. Verified on staging: trust(0x742b7dbb, score group)=0, yet a regular (unconstrained) findPath delivers 8 steps of other tokens to this sink, so the sink IS reachable — the no-path result is caused purely by the unsatisfiable delivery constraint, not an unreachable sink. Before the fix the pathfinder degraded the emptied toTokens filter to 'no constraint' and returned that 8-step regular path (delivering tokens the caller never requested); the fix forces no path when an explicit toTokens narrows to empty. The dbStateAssertion proves the sink does not trust the group at the pinned block. P11 / toTokens-enforcement.
